_note: Demo seed content for the live Discord showcase. Channels: #gaming (casual game chat) and #japan-trip (planning a group trip). One friend group of students. English; #japan-trip mixes Cantonese + English (HK student code-switching). Deep multi-turn threads + breadth. Posted via demo/seed_discord.py --fixtures demo/discord_demo_conversations.json. Discord server channels must be named 'gaming' and 'japan-trip'. Same schema as the Slack fixtures.

channels.name: gaming
channels.topic: What we're playing, debates, and co-op nights
channels.purpose: Casual gaming chat — recs, hot takes, and squad-up plans

channels.name: japan-trip
channels.topic: Planning the group trip to Japan
channels.purpose: Itinerary, food, budget, and logistics for the Japan trip

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Settle it: best video game ever made? Give me your pick AND your criteria 🎮

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Half-Life 2. It basically invented modern FPS pacing + physics — everything after it copied the homework.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: The Witcher 3. Best side quests in any game — they're better than most games' MAIN stories. The Bloody Baron arc alone makes the case.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Elden Ring. Open-world done right — no map markers spoon-feeding you, discovery IS the reward. FromSoft at their peak.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Zelda: Breath of the Wild. It rewrote the open-world rulebook — climb anything, solve a puzzle however you want. Physics-sandbox GOAT.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Disco Elysium. No combat, all writing — and it's the most I've ever cared about my choices in a game. A different kind of masterpiece entirely.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Tetris. Be serious. Perfect game, 40 years old, design still unbeatable. Everything else here is decoration 😌

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Hollow Knight. A $15 indie that out-arts most AAA — the hand-drawn world + that soundtrack… chef's kiss.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: No consensus as always 😂 but notice 'criteria' split us cleanly: innovation people vs writing people vs pure-design people. Great answers all round.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: What's everyone actually playing this week?

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Replaying Elden Ring with the DLC. Send help, Messmer is dismantling me 🗡️

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Balatro. I told myself 'one more run' at midnight. It is now 3am. This is a cry for help.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Stardew Valley to decompress before finals. Pure serotonin farming, no notes.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Animal Crossing — redesigning my island for the 5th time instead of studying 🍃

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Factorio. The factory must grow. I have not slept. The factory must grow.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Chess.com, as always. Does that count 😅

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Genuine debate: PC, console, or Steam Deck as your MAIN — and why?

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: PC. Mods, backwards compatibility, and I already need it for coursework — one device does everything.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Console for the couch + zero config. I want to press power and play, not debug GPU drivers at 11pm.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Steam Deck genuinely changed my life — whole backlog, on the train, on a plane. Handheld + dock = best of both. https://www.steamdeck.com/

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Steam Deck for me too, mostly for indies. Battery's the only catch on the big AAA stuff.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Whatever runs Tetris 🧱

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Co-op night this Friday? Need to pick something we can all jump into.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Lethal Company — terrifying and hilarious, perfect for a group call.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Or Overcooked if we want to ruin friendships in a fun way 😂

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: It Takes Two if it ends up being just two of us — genuinely one of the best co-op games ever made.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Can we do Jackbox as a warmup so non-gamers (me 🙋) can actually join?

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Plan locked: Jackbox warmup → Lethal Company → Overcooked if we still like each other. Friday 8pm 🎮

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Underrated indie everyone in here should play?

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Outer Wilds. Do NOT look anything up. Just play it blind. That's all I'll say 🌌

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Hades — best roguelike, and the story works BECAUSE you die a lot. Supergiant are geniuses.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Tunic. Looks like a cute Zelda, secretly a brutal puzzle box — the in-game 'manual' mechanic is genius.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Inscryption. Going in blind is mandatory — it keeps reinventing itself in ways I can't spoil.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Cocoon. Short, no words, pure puzzle elegance — a perfect weekend gem.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Confession: my Steam backlog just hit 200+ games. Sales are a trap and I keep falling in.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: 200 is rookie numbers. I own games still in the (digital) shrink wrap 😭

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: The backlog isn't a to-do list, it's a museum of who you thought you'd be.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Ken that's the most profound thing ever said in this server 😂

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: I just rebuy games I've already finished. The backlog stays pristine and untouched. No notes.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Worlds (League) was unreal this year. Even as a total casual, the final game had me yelling at my screen.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Hot take: the hardest boss in any game is still Sekiro's final fight. I have not fully recovered.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Comfort game I always come back to: Minecraft. Put on a podcast, build a dumb little house, achieve inner peace.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Game soundtrack that lives in my head rent-free: NieR Automata. I genuinely teared up at a video game, no shame.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: If you could wipe your memory to replay ONE game completely fresh, which would it be?

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Outer Wilds, instantly. The whole game is discovery — knowing anything ruins it.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: The Witcher 3 — 150 hours I'd happily live through again from scratch.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Anyone else physically incapable of NOT picking the hardest difficulty and then suffering for it 😩

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: New FromSoft game got teased. I am already emotionally preparing to die a thousand times and pay full price for the privilege.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Speedrun rabbit-hole alert: watched a 20-min doc on a Mario 64 world record and it's somehow 2am again.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Controller or keyboard+mouse — and you're not allowed to say 'depends on the game' 😆

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Controller. Vibes, couch, ergonomics. KBM feels like work.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: KBM for anything with aiming, controller for everything else. (sorry Ken, you said no fence-sitting and I did it anyway)

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Controller — but I will die on the hill that fighting games need a proper arcade stick.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Cozy game recs for when life is A Lot? Currently rotating Unpacking + A Short Hike 🍃

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: A Short Hike is perfect — finished it in an afternoon and felt genuinely better afterwards. Adding Unpacking to the list.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Remaster/port we actually NEED: a proper Bloodborne PC version at 60fps. It being locked to old hardware is a crime.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Bloodborne 60fps on PC would completely end my productivity. Please no. (please yes)

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Most replayable game for me is Slay the Spire — the perfect 'one more run' loop, basically Balatro before Balatro.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Mobile game that's secretly excellent: Marvel Snap. 3-minute matches, surprisingly deep deckbuilding. Dangerous on the commute.

C_DEMO_GAMING.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_GAMING.content: Server, assemble — let's do a retro night sometime. Emulated N64 Mario Kart, winner keeps the crown 🏆

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Ok let's ACTUALLY plan the Japan trip 🇯🇵 First big question: when do we go?

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Late March–early April for cherry blossoms, obviously. Catch: it's peak season — expensive and very crowded.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Autumn (November) is underrated — the koyo (fall leaves) are stunning, weather's ideal, and it's noticeably cheaper than sakura season.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Whatever we pick, AVOID late July–August. The humidity is genuinely brutal — I've heard horror stories of melting in Kyoto.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Counterpoint: if anyone has a non-negotiable (a specific festival, snow in Hokkaido) that decides the date for us. What are our must-haves?

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: I just want cherry blossoms for the photos honestly 🌸 willing to pay the crowd tax for it.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Leaning early April then — sakura, accept the crowds. Let's lock dates before flight prices jump.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Itinerary — which cities and how many days? Proposing 10–12 days total.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Tokyo (4 nights) + Kyoto (3) + Osaka (2) is the classic golden route for a first trip. Hard to go wrong.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Add Nara as a day trip from Kyoto or Osaka — the bowing deer + Todai-ji's giant Buddha are worth half a day easily.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: One night in Hakone for Mt Fuji views + an onsen. The Romancecar makes it a nice break between Tokyo and Kyoto.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Please let's NOT over-pack the schedule. One 'rest / just wander' day per city or we'll be dead by day 6.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Can we keep a Tokyo afternoon free for shopping + teamLab? Those genuinely need a few hours each.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Draft: Tokyo 4 → Hakone 1 → Kyoto 3 (+ Nara day) → Osaka 2 = 11 days. Built in wander time. Adjustable.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: That looks great. Hiroshima/Miyajima is tempting but it's far — let's save it for trip #2 and not rush this one.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Is the JR Pass still worth it after the price hike? We need to actually do the math. https://www.japanrailpass.net/

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: For Tokyo↔Kyoto↔Osaka round-trip the 7-day pass roughly breaks even now. It used to be a no-brainer; post-hike it's marginal.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Regional passes might beat the nationwide one for our exact route — the Kansai-area passes etc. Depends on the final itinerary.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Within cities you're on the metro anyway (Suica/ICOCA), so the pass really only earns its keep on the shinkansen legs.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Let's price the exact legs once dates are locked, THEN decide pass vs individual tickets. Don't buy it blind.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Agreed — I'll make a spreadsheet of the legs so we can compare properly 📊

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: The truly important question: FOOD 🍜 what are the non-negotiable must-eats?

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Proper tonkotsu ramen, sushi at the Tsukiji outer market, okonomiyaki in Osaka, and konbini egg sandwiches unironically (they're elite).

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Conveyor-belt sushi at least once for the chaos. And an izakaya night — that's where the real culture is.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Osaka is THE food city — takoyaki, kushikatsu, Dotonbori at night. We should weight the Osaka days around eating.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Veg note from me 🙏 I'll research vegetarian spots — traditional dashi is in almost everything, so it takes a bit of planning.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Shojin ryori (Buddhist temple cuisine) in Kyoto is fully vegetarian AND an experience — that's one meal sorted for you, Mei.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Adding one splurge dinner to the plan — let's budget it in now so we don't talk ourselves out of it later.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Budget reality check — what's our per-day target, and hostels vs hotels vs ryokan?

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Mix it: business hotels in the cities (cheap, spotless, central) + ONE ryokan night for the experience (onsen + kaiseki dinner).

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Capsule hotel for one night too, just to say we did it. They're ~¥3-4k and weirdly comfortable.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Rough budget ex-flights: ¥12-15k/day per person covers mid hotels + food + transit if we're not reckless about shopping.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Book the ryokan EARLY though — the good ones with a private onsen sell out months ahead, especially in sakura season.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: I'll start a shared budget doc. Flights + JR + the ryokan are the big rocks; the rest is flexible.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Bucket-list experiences — what are we 100% doing no matter what?

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Fushimi Inari at sunrise (before the crowds), teamLab in Tokyo, and one onsen-town night. https://www.teamlab.art/

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Shibuya Crossing + Shinjuku at night for the neon, and Akihabara for the pure sensory chaos.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: A clear Mt Fuji view day — either from Hakone or the Kawaguchiko side. We build in a buffer day in case it's cloudy.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Super Nintendo World at Universal Osaka if we want a gamer day 🎮 (extremely relevant to the other channel).

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Ghibli Museum in Tokyo 🌿 but tickets are date-stamped and sell out instantly, so we book the SECOND they release. https://www.ghibli-museum.jp/en/

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Logistics: get Suica/ICOCA cards on arrival (or add to Apple Wallet) — tap for trains AND konbini, no fumbling for coins.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Pocket wifi vs eSIM? I vote eSIM (Ubigi/Airalo) — nothing to return, cheaper, works the second you land.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Cash matters more than you'd expect — some smaller places + shrines are cash-only. Withdraw at 7-Eleven ATMs, they take foreign cards.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Etiquette so we're not 'those tourists': no eating while walking, keep quiet on trains, no tipping, shoes off where indicated 🙏

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Language: download Google Translate offline + the camera mode for menus. Learning 'sumimasen' and 'arigatou gozaimasu' goes a long way.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: The shinkansen itself is an experience — reserve seats on the RIGHT side Tokyo→Kyoto for the Mt Fuji view 🗻

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Jet lag plan: force ourselves onto local time on day 1 — sunlight, no napping. Day 1 is always a write-off otherwise.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Group-size note: 6+ of us makes restaurants tricky (lots seat 4). We'll need to split for some meals + book ahead where we can.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Optional side quest: a couple of us could hit some anime pilgrimage spots. Honestly this is half a gamer/anime trip already 😄

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Packing PSA: comfortable walking shoes are non-negotiable — we'll easily do 20k+ steps a day.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Flights — set a price alert NOW. YYZ→Tokyo tends to dip around 3 months out.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Being flexible by a day or two saves a lot. Also HND > NRT for arrival — much closer to central Tokyo.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Watch for a layover via somewhere fun (Seoul/Taipei) if it's much cheaper and we fancy a bonus day on the way.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: I'll put a Google Flights alert in the group doc. Let's lock dates this week so we can pounce on a good fare.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Genuinely SO excited, this is actually happening 🇯🇵🌸 thank you Mei for herding all of us

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Day-trip debate: Nikko vs Kamakura from Tokyo? Both are great but we likely only have time for one.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Kamakura — closer, the Great Buddha + a beach + easy half-day. Nikko's shrines are stunning but it's a full committed day.

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Kamakura for time-efficiency, agreed. Save Nikko for the autumn-leaves version of this trip 🍁

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Souvenir budget warning: Don Quijote (Donki) and the konbini WILL take all your money. Set yourself a limit 😅

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Final logistics doc is up — dates poll, flight alert, budget sheet, itinerary draft. Everyone fill in the dates poll by Friday 🙏

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 講起手信,我哋買咩好呀?家人朋友都要帶返啲 🎁 順便計埋個budget

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 抹茶味KitKat必買,仲有東京香蕉(Tokyo Banana)。講真,Don Quijote一次過掃晒最方便又抵。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Royce生巧克力勁好食,但要保冷,留到最後一兩日先買。機場都有得買㗎。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 藥妝先係無底洞 😂 朋友order咗眼藥水、休足時間同白色戀人曲奇俾我帶。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 我已經開咗個souvenir list喺shared doc,大家加返自己要買嘅落去,唔好臨走先嚟搵 🙏

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Agreed 👍 記得留個半空嘅喼俾手信,我上次咁先夠位裝返嚟。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 四月到底要帶咩衫?查過話日夜溫差好大,日頭暖夜晚幾凍 🌸

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Layering係王道 — 薄外套加長袖,日頭除得夜晚著返。我會帶埋件摺得細嘅羽絨。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 帶把細遮,四月時不時落雨。仲有對舒服嘅波鞋,一日行20k步唔係講笑。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 想影相靚啲就帶一兩件靚色衫,配sakura背景超出片 📸

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 插頭記得帶轉換器,日本係Type A。仲有尿袋(power bank),成日開Google Map好快無電。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Update:我check過,JR Pass而家可以網上reserve seat,唔使去到車站先排隊撲飛 ✅

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 好正!咁我哋一confirm到date,就即刻reserve定shinkansen嘅位。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 機票price alert響喇 — YYZ→HND 四月頭有個唔錯嘅價,pull the trigger? ✈️

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 幾錢?好價就要快手,sakura季啲機票升得好癲。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 連稅大約CAD 1450 round trip,HND直航。平過我之前見嘅成兩舊水。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 呢個價入得喇,HND又近市區,慳返去Narita嗰段車程嘅時間同錢。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 我open咗條date poll喺doc,大家今晚之前vote咗佢,聽日就book ⏰

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Voted ✅ 我OK四月二號去、十二號返,啱啱好sakura full bloom。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 再補幾間食店落list:大阪蟹道樂、京都湯豆腐、東京一蘭拉麵 🍜

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 湯豆腐啱我!素食者終於唔使次次淨係食白飯 😂🙏

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 宇治(Uji)嘅抹茶甜品必試,啲parfait靚到唔捨得食 🍵

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Convenience store food唔好睇小,Lawson炸雞同7-11蛋三文治係傳奇 🐔

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 我一定要食一餐和牛,人生總要奢侈一次 🥩

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_RAY
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 提提大家:日本好多地方仲係cash only,記得換定現金,或者帶張可以海外提款嘅card 💴

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 已經喺doc加咗『落機第一件事:整Suica + 換現金』喺checklist度 ✅

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 夜晚想影靚相:道頓堀同新宿啲霓虹勁出片,帶埋個mini三腳架 🌃

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Final reminder:大家check埋本passport,有效期最少要過行程後六個月 🛂

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_TIN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Checked ✅ 另外記得買旅遊保險,我上次就係靠保險claim返筆機票錢。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_SAM
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Btw想睇相撲嘅,五月東京先有場,我哋四月去趕唔切,留返下次喇 🤼

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_JAS
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: OK summary for the doc in English: dates ~Apr 2–12, HND flights, JR Pass + seats reserved, ryokan & Ghibli booked early. 我哋真係搞掂緊喇 🎉

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_WING
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Master doc updated — itinerary、budget、packing list、souvenir list、checklist 全部喺度,個link置咗頂 📌

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_MEI
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: Thank you Wing 🙏 大家今晚記低自己嘅must-do,下次meeting排返優先次序。

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_CARMEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 真係好excited 😭🌸 等咗咁耐終於成行,一齊去日本喇!

C_DEMO_JAPAN.author: U_KEN
C_DEMO_JAPAN.content: 次次都係Mei同Wing搞掂晒,多謝晒你哋兩個 🙇 see you in Japan!
